title: Ginger Scallion Noodles
intro: David Chang's ginger scallion sauce is legendary. The Thermomix chops it into a punchy, glossy sauce in seconds — toss through hot noodles and dinner's done.
steps: Place the spring onions and ginger in the bowl and chop 4 sec / 0°C / speed 5. Scrape down.
Add the oil and warm 3 min / 70°C / speed 1 to mellow the raw bite.
Add the soy, vinegar, sesame oil and sugar and mix 5 sec / 0°C / speed 3.
Toss the warm sauce through hot cooked noodles and serve straight away; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Gently warming the sauce takes the raw edge off the ginger and onion.
Brilliant over rice, greens or chicken too.

faq.q: Can I make the sauce ahead?
faq.a: Yes — it keeps a few days in the fridge and gets better as it sits.

faq.q: Is this the original David Chang recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by David Chang and ginger scallion noodles; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Ginger Scallion Noodles in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of David Chang-style ginger scallion noodles: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
